message:
test(gui/glyphcache): pin atlas-rect lookup invariants — UV bounds, memoization, distinctness, scale (ft-1memj.28/C, ft-p52l5)

The pre-existing test_atlas_rect_lookup_returns_expected_uv_tuple_for_first_block_glyph
covered exactly one case: the first block glyph lands at pixel origin
(1, 1) with the expected UV tuple in a 128-wide atlas. That catches a
totally broken allocator but not the invariants the renderer actually
relies on when sampling the atlas.

Add four targeted unit tests around atlas rect lookup in glyphcache.rs:

* atlas_rect_lookup_uv_coords_stay_within_unit_square
    The renderer samples via UV in [0, 1]. Any glyph whose
    texture_coords() exit that range would either wrap or sample
    garbage — a visible rendering bug.
* atlas_rect_lookup_repeated_glyph_returns_same_rect
    Looking up the same glyph twice must return the same rect AND the
    same UV tuple. This is the memoization contract the per-frame quad
    allocator depends on; breaking it would burn atlas space and
    trigger early eviction.
* atlas_rect_lookup_distinct_glyphs_get_distinct_rects
    Two different cached entries must occupy non-overlapping rects.
    A collision would render glyph A's pixels in glyph B's cell. Uses
    two cached_color entries (cheap and exercises the same Atlas
    allocator that backs glyphs).
* atlas_rect_lookup_uv_scales_inversely_with_atlas_size
    Same source glyph in a 64-wide vs 256-wide atlas must produce UV
    coordinates that scale by the atlas-size ratio (4×). Both land at
    pixel origin (1, 1) with the 1-pixel border the Atlas leaves for
    filtering, so the small atlas's UV equals exactly 4× the large
    atlas's UV. Pins the pixel→UV math.

No production change. Verified via:

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target \
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    cargo test -p frankenterm-gui atlas_rect

5 atlas_rect tests pass (the original + 4 new). Closes the third
unit-test row in ft-1memj.28's GPU-unrequired list.
